english: pf. skraṅs (skrangs), to swell, *sraṅs-soṅ* (srangs song) it is swollen, a tumour, a bile, a weal has formed itself W.; skraṅs-po (skrangs po) Sch. a swelling, tumour; skraṅs-búr (skrangs bur) Sch. an abscess not yet open.

1. Ssk. गुल्म Cs.: a fleshy etc excrescence in the abdomen, a concretion under the skin, in the bowels, womb etc., Sch. also: a swelling of the glands. Wise (Commentary on Hindoo Medicine) says, that very different diseases are comprised under the term gulma, tumours of the pylorus, partial enlargements of the liver, diseases of the large intestines, fixed and moveable swellings; - perhaps also herniae, which I did not find mentioned elsewhere. - In S. g. I found skran-nád (skran nad) described as a consequence of great fatigue and want of breath, and skran-gzér (skran gzer) as pain in consequence of suppressed winds. - 2. rdo-skrán (rdo skran), bad-skrán (bad skran), two sorts of steatite C.
Cs.: 'to beat the ground with one's feet,' to stamp, tread, cf. kráb-pa (krab pa); Lex.: bró-skrab-pa (bro skrab pa), to dance.
1. Cs. to conduct (?) 2. W. *śri-ċe* (shri ce) f. dkri-ba (dkri ba).

pf. bskrus (bskrus) ft. bskru (bskru), Sch.: to wait; the latter would suit well in a passage of Mil., perh. also in zás-la skru (zas la skru) of the Lexx.; but šiṅ-skrus-pa (shing skrus pa) Lexx. remains unexplained.
pf. & ft. bskrun (bskrun) to produce, fruits Mil., a root of virtue (v. rtsá-ba (rtsa ba)) Stg.
meat, resp. viz. when spoken of as the food of respected persons.
= dkróg-pa (dkrog pa), perh. also f. skrág-pa (skrag pa). Lexx. ḍá-ru skróg-po (da ru skrog po) to beat the drum: W. *kopóṅ śróg-ċe* (ko pong shrog ce) to play on the guitar.
pf. & ft. bskrad (bskrad) to expel, drive out, eject, out of the country Dzl., Mil.; to deprive of cast; *śrád-de taṅ ċe* (shrad de tang ce) to expel a thief publicly out of the village W.
words beginning with these letters will in most cases be found arranged under rk.. (rk..) and sk.. (sk..).
Ssk. कषाय, astringent, as to taste, Cs. erron.: bitter.

Ssk. कल्प, a kalpa, a fabulous period of time; the fantastical reveries of the Buddhists concerning this subject v. Kō. I. 266, also Will. under kalpa bskál-pa čén-po (bskal pa chen po) the great kalpa; bár-(gyi) bskal-pa (bar gyi bskal pa) the intervening or middle 'kalpa'; bsk. bzáṅ-po (bsk. bzang po) the happy, blessed period, viz. in which Buddhas appear; bskál-pa ṅán-pa (bskal pa ngan pa) the bad 'kalpa'; bskal-mé (bskal me) conflagration of the universe.
1. the letter k', aspirated, like c in 'call'. - 2. numerical figure: two, k'a-pa (kha pa) the second volume.

I. additional syllable, = ka (ka), but less frequent. - II. in compounds instead of k'á-ba (kha ba) bitter and k'á-ba (kha ba) snow; for the latter signification it is in W. the only form existing. III. i. o. k'ag (khag) part. k'a gnyis-su (kha gnyis su) into two parts (e. g. to cleave) Stg.; *k'a-gháṅ* (kha ghang) <
